reasoning: This is a correct and desirable property — if bits 5 and 6 are already clear, ANDing with the complement mask is identity. Verified by `by (bit_vector)` in the lemma body.
Standard bitvector fact: `v & (1 << i)` is always either 0 or `1 << i`. This is a correct mathematical consequence of single-bit masking, not a spec gap.
Correct and desirable — MASK_NEG_DIRTY_ACCESS only clears bits 5 and 6, so bit 0 (present bit) is preserved. Verified by `by (bit_vector)`.
Correct and desirable — the entire purpose of MASK_NEG_DIRTY_ACCESS is to clear bit 5 (access). This confirms the mask works as intended.
Correct and desirable — the entire purpose of MASK_NEG_DIRTY_ACCESS is to clear bit 6 (dirty). This confirms the mask works as intended.
